docs.title: DataSpringFlow 󰏗
docs.text: Light-weight Un-intrusive dataset metadata management tool powered by Rust and Python.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page documentation is automatically generated from the .pyi type stubs.

docs.title: pydsf.DSFService
docs.text:
Main service entrypoint for managing, querying, and verifying datasets.

Provides core concurrency fencing (mark_status), cryptographic hashing (update_merkle), and topological DAG verification across storage backends.

Example 
 service = DSFService()
 # Erect fence -> mutate disk -> seal changes -> tear down fence
 service.mark_status("data@v1", BusyStatus.Modifying)
 # ... modify disk files ...
 service.update_merkle("data@v1")
 service.mark_status("data@v1", BusyStatus.Free)

docs.title: __init__()
docs.text:
Initializes the DSF service by auto-detecting default backend hierarchy.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If the backend architecture fails to initialize or connect.

Example 
service = DSFService()

docs.title: check_is_referenced(target_id)
docs.text:
Finds all downstream datasets that depend on the specified dataset.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default target_id str

The identifier of the parent dataset to check for references.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[ScopedId]

A list of ScopedId objects representing dependent datasets.

Raises:

Type Description IOError

If querying the backend graph fails.

Example 
 refs = service.check_is_referenced("raw_corpus@v1.0")
 if refs:
     print("Cannot modify safely: other datasets depend on this!")

docs.title: delete_metadata(id, force=False, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Deletes a dataset's metadata records from the specified database.

To ensure safety during long cleanup tasks, consider marking the dataset with BusyStatus.Deleting before removing physical disk files or unregistering metadata.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The dataset identifier to delete.

required force bool

If True, forces deletion even if downstream datasets depend on this.

False target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional target backend to execute deletion against.

None

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If deletion fails or if dependencies exist without force=True.

Example 
 service.mark_status("old_data@v0.1", BusyStatus.Deleting)
 import shutil; shutil.rmtree("./data/old_data")  # Remove disk files
 service.delete_metadata("old_data@v0.1", force=True)

docs.title: list_all_metadata()
docs.text:
Lists all dataset metadata registered across available backends.

Returns:

Type Description list[ScopedMetaData]

A list of all discovered ScopedMetaData objects.

Raises:

Type Description IOError

If querying the backend databases fails.

docs.title: mark_status(id, status, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Sets or releases a concurrency fence (busy status) on a target dataset.

Essential for preventing concurrent modification collisions or race conditions while mutating disk files or computing Merkle trees.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The dataset identifier ("name@tag").

required status BusyStatus

The target concurrency status (e.g., BusyStatus.Modifying or BusyStatus.Free).

required target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional specific backend to execute against.

None

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If setting the status in the target storage backend fails.

Example 
 service.mark_status("data@v1", BusyStatus.Modifying)
 # Perform disk modifications...
 service.update_merkle("data@v1")
 service.mark_status("data@v1", BusyStatus.Free)

docs.title: query_meta(id, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Queries metadata for a specific dataset identifier across backends.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The formatted dataset identifier (e.g., "imagenet@v1.0").

required target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional specific backend to query.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[ScopedMetaData]

A list of ScopedMetaData objects matching the identifier.

Example 
metas = service.query_meta("imagenet@v1.0")
if metas:
    print("Dataset found.")

docs.title: register(name, tag, path, script_path, owner_nickname=None, dependencies=None, description_path=None, target_backend=None, force_heal=False)
docs.text:
Registers a new dataset into the DataSpringFlow ecosystem.

For large directories, this operation initializes disk Merkle hashing. It verifies dependency DAG health before committing the initial records.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default name str

Name of the dataset (without tag or '@').

required tag str

Version tag (e.g., "v1.0").

required path str

String path to target dataset directory or files.

required script_path str

String path to generating script.

required owner_nickname Optional[str]

Optional nickname (formats to linux_user$nick).

None dependencies Optional[list[str]]

Optional list of required parent IDs ("name@tag").

None description_path Optional[str]

Optional markdown description path.

None target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional backend destination for saving.

None force_heal bool

If True, forces healing existing broken dependency records.

False

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If dataset registration or initialization fails.

Example 
 service.register(
     name="clean_corpus", tag="v1.0",
     path="./data/corpus", script_path="./scripts/clean.py",
     dependencies=["raw_corpus@v1.0"], force_heal=True
 )

docs.title: update_merkle(id, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Recalculates and seals the Merkle tree hash for a registered dataset.

Essential step before releasing a BusyStatus.Modifying concurrency fence. It commits the new disk state to storage so subsequent verifications return Healthy.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The dataset identifier ("name@tag").

required target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional target backend where the dataset resides.

None

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If recalculating or saving the Merkle tree fails.

Example 
 service.mark_status("corpus@v1", BusyStatus.Modifying)
 # Alter disk files...
 service.update_merkle("corpus@v1")
 service.mark_status("corpus@v1", BusyStatus.Free)

docs.title: verify_deep(id, show_diff=False, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Performs deep topological verification of target and its dependency DAG.

Respects concurrency fences: if any upstream dependency is marked with a non-read busy status, verification short-circuits to report the lock without hashing.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The dataset identifier to verify.

required show_diff bool

If True, logs file-level diffs when corruption is detected.

False target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional specific backend to run verification against.

None

Returns:

Type Description DataSetVerifyRes

A DataSetVerifyRes with the state of target and all dependencies.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If the deep verification process fails to execute.

Example 
 res = service.verify_deep("clean_corpus@v1.0")
 if res.status == DatasetStatus.Healthy:
     print("DAG verified and ready.")

docs.title: verify_self(id, show_diff=False, target_backend=None)
docs.text:
Performs verification strictly on target dataset, ignoring upstream states.

Short-circuits immediately if the dataset is under an active concurrency fence (such as BusyStatus.Modifying), avoiding collisions with ongoing disk IO.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default id str

The dataset identifier to verify.

required show_diff bool

If True, logs file-level diffs when corruption is detected.

False target_backend Optional[BackendAddr]

Optional specific backend to run verification against.

None

Returns:

Type Description DataSetVerifyRes

A DataSetVerifyRes containing only the health of target dataset.

Raises:

Type Description RuntimeError

If self verification process fails to execute.

Example 
 res = service.verify_self("clean_corpus@v1.0")
 if res.status == DatasetStatus.Healthy:
     print("Disk data is verified and safe for concurrent read.")

docs.title: pydsf.MetaData
docs.text:
Metadata snapshot representing a registered dataset in DataSpringFlow.

Attributes:

Name Type Description name str

The dataset name (without tag).

tag str

The version tag of the dataset (e.g., "v1.0").

hash str

The cryptographic hex hash representing the dataset contents.

path str

Absolute or relative string path to the actual dataset files.

description_path str

String path to the descriptive markdown or text document.

script_path str

String path to the processing or generation script.

owner str

The username or nickname of the dataset owner (formatted as "user$nick").

dependencies list[str]

List of formatted dataset IDs ("name@tag") that this depends on.

merkle_tree_path str

String path to the serialized Merkle tree file.

Example 
 meta = service.query_meta("imagenet@v1.0")[0].metadata
 print(f"Dataset path: {meta.path}")

docs.title: id()
docs.text:
Returns the formatted dataset identifier.

Returns:

Type Description str

The dataset identifier string in the format "name@tag".

Example 
 meta = service.query_meta("imagenet@v1.0")[0].metadata
 assert meta.id() == "imagenet@v1.0"

docs.title: pydsf.DatasetStatus
docs.text:
Represents the overall health or verification status of a dataset.

Attributes:

Name Type Description Healthy DatasetStatus

Dataset is intact, verified, and free of corruption.

Broken DatasetStatus

Dataset files have failed verification or are missing.

BrokenDeps DatasetStatus

Target dataset is fine, but one or more upstream dependencies are broken.

Unverified DatasetStatus

Dataset has been registered or modified but not yet verified.
